behaviors.description: Cache keys generated from identical query parameters are always identical, and keys from different parameters never collide.
behaviors.justification: Failure mode: logic error in parameter canonicalization/hash construction. Required setup: call key-generation function with controlled parameter sets (same params in different orders, different params) and compare outputs. Rejected higher levels: behavior text is only about deterministic key derivation from query parameters; no module handoff, OS/network/subprocess, or AI dependency is required.
behaviors.test_description: Unit-test the key builder with a table of parameter cases: identical semantic inputs must produce identical keys; distinct inputs from a large representative corpus must produce distinct keys. Include order/format normalization cases to catch canonicalization bugs.

behaviors.description: The TTL for a cache entry is computed correctly based on the data category's configured freshness rule (e.g., user profiles = 5 minutes, product catalog = 1 hour).
behaviors.justification: Failure mode: logic/config-lookup error (wrong category mapping, unit conversion mistakes). Required setup: in-memory freshness-rule config passed to TTL computation code. Rejected higher levels: behavior text focuses on computing TTL from configured rules, not on reading real files/services or cross-process effects.
behaviors.test_description: Unit-test TTL computation with categories and expected durations (e.g., 5m, 1h), including boundary/unknown-category handling and minute-vs-hour conversion checks.

behaviors.description: When memory usage exceeds the configured threshold, the eviction strategy switches from LRU to LFU as specified in the configuration.
behaviors.justification: Failure mode: module handoff/wiring error between memory-usage signal, configuration, and strategy selector. Required setup: wire cache manager + config + usage monitor component and drive usage across threshold. Integration-brake: no explicit need for real OS/process memory metrics in behavior text; an in-process usage provider can expose the real boundary here, so System is not required. No AI dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Integration-test composed cache components with an in-process memory-usage source: verify strategy is LRU below threshold and switches to LFU when threshold is crossed, honoring configured threshold values.

behaviors.description: A write to the underlying PostgreSQL table invalidates all cache entries whose keys match the affected rows.
behaviors.justification: Failure mode: environment/external-system error at DB boundary (real PostgreSQL write/change propagation to invalidation path). Required setup: real PostgreSQL instance and real write operation triggering invalidation mechanism. Rejected lower levels: mocks can hide trigger/listener/transaction timing issues implied by 'write to the underlying PostgreSQL table'. No AI dependency.
behaviors.test_description: System-test with a real PostgreSQL instance: seed cache entries, perform real table write/update/delete affecting specific rows, then assert matching cache keys are invalidated and non-matching keys remain.

behaviors.description: The eviction policy removes the correct entries — least-recently-used under LRU, least-frequently-used under LFU — when the cache is full.
behaviors.justification: Failure mode: algorithmic logic error in eviction ordering/accounting (recency counters, frequency counters, tie handling). Required setup: in-memory cache/policy object with deterministic access patterns. Rejected higher levels: behavior text is policy correctness at cache-full condition, not external infra or multi-module orchestration.
behaviors.test_description: Unit-test eviction policy with deterministic sequences: for LRU, assert oldest unaccessed entry is evicted after specific access order; for LFU, assert lowest-access-count entry is evicted after controlled hit counts when capacity is reached.
